notes: task-estimate-marker for kiro-cli. The agent emits its own progress estimate in-band as a hidden HTML comment in assistant text — <!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":M} --> — advanced 0→1→2→3 across four working turns, and irrlicht surfaces task_estimate {total_rounds:3, completed_rounds:M, updated_at} + a monotone-non-increasing task_completion_eta on the session metrics WHILE working, then drops both fields at ready. DAEMON HANDLES THIS: the kiro parser (parser.go:62-68) feeds tailer.ScanTaskEstimate(text, ev.Timestamp) the FULL assistant text block BEFORE the 200-rune display truncation, sets ParsedEvent.TaskEstimate; the shared scan lives in core/pkg/tailer/taskestimate_scan.go and the downstream pipeline (tailer persistence → domain TaskEstimate/TaskCompletionEta projection at elapsed/completed → API metrics, suppressed off the working state) is shared with claudecode/aider. The marker survives in the transcript: kiro writes raw assistant text verbatim into the <uuid>.jsonl (live-append confirmed, FINDINGS.md §2), with no markdown render or comment-stripping between the model's reply and the file. The marker rule is delivered IN the prompt (no instruction file is seeded) — mirroring the aider cell — so there is no seed_instruction driver gap. EMISSION RISK (provisional): whether kiro's model re-emits the marker turn-over-turn with climbing completed_rounds is a model-behavior question only a live recording settles (the 2026-05-31 experiment was claudecode/sonnet only). SESSION IDENTITY (kiro-specific): kiro is FilesUnderRoot{.kiro/sessions/cli} keyed by a transcript UUID, written LAZILY (only after the first prompt). The on-time ready birth is the pre-session proc-<PID> row; the UUID session reconciles in (new session_id, born ready) when the lazy transcript appears, and the working/ready arc plays out on the UUID session. PID is bound by CWD match (DiscoverPIDByCWD). Recorded with the INTERACTIVE TUI driver (kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools), NOT headless --no-interactive: headless writes NO session file and is invisible to the daemon (FINDINGS.md §7); the REPL must outlive the observation window so each working→ready settle and the final ready-suppression are captured. OBSERVATIONS: the kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar holds credits + model_id, input/output_token_count=0; the parser does not read it), so the observations vector asserts model unknown (omitted), tokens_nonzero=false, cost_nonzero=false — the scenario's rate/ETA assertions are still observable because they derive from the marker + the daemon's own elapsed wall-clock, not agent-reported tokens. This expected.jsonl is the documented target the recording validates against.

text: Pre-session (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of the interactive launch (UNPINNED anchor so a transient presession row can't steal the birth)
UUID session reconciles (different session_id) after the lazy <uuid>.jsonl appears on first prompt, born ready
PID bound to the UUID session (DiscoverPIDByCWD on the kiro-cli chat parent's cwd)

Round-1 prompt → working. The agent emits the first marker (completed_rounds:0/total_rounds:3); EXPECTED: task_estimate.total_rounds=3, task_estimate.completed_rounds=0 present on the working session's metrics.

Round-1 turn settles to ready (text-only assistant message = turn_done). The session is no longer working, so task_estimate/task_completion_eta are suppressed here — they reappear on the next working turn.

Round-2 prompt → working; marker advances to completed_rounds:1. EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=1 (≤ total_rounds, non-decreasing vs round 1), updated_at advances, and task_completion_eta is present and ≤ its round-1 value (ETA burns down).

Round-3 prompt → working; marker advances to completed_rounds:2. EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=2 (non-decreasing), updated_at advances, task_completion_eta ≤ the round-2 value (continued burndown).
Final working turn → marker reaches completed_rounds:3 == total_rounds (the agent reports the task done). EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=3=total_rounds on the working metrics.
After a ≥10s trailing idle the session settles to ready. EXPECTED: task_estimate AND task_completion_eta are ABSENT once the session is no longer working (suppressed-by-state, client gate).
TUI torn down once after the final settle → a process_exited on the UUID session; state immediately before teardown is ready. Final state: ready.
